metadata.description: Maps each deduplicated threat to its discovering Phase B specialists for confidence analysis

TECH-H001.title: Router._validateDecimals underflows when baseDecimals > quoteDecimals, blocking valid pool creation
TECH-H001.duplicate_group_description: Router._validateDecimals uint8 unsigned subtraction underflows when baseDecimals > quoteDecimals, blocking valid pool creation

TECH-H002.title: MagicLP.flashLoan allows fee-free flash loans when borrower returns both tokens
TECH-H002.duplicate_group_description: MagicLP.flashLoan charges zero fees when borrower returns both tokens such that both balances >= reserves

TECH-H003.title: MagicLP.sellBase/sellQuote use tx.origin for fee rate lookup enabling fee manipulation
TECH-H003.duplicate_group_description: MagicLP sellBase/sellQuote/flashLoan use tx.origin instead of msg.sender for fee rate determination, enabling fee manipulation

TECH-H005.title: MagicLP flashLoan and swap mtFee extraction from pool reserves causes systematic LP value erosion
TECH-H005.duplicate_group_description: Maintainer fee (mtFee) extraction during swaps/flash loans causes systematic target-reserve drift eroding LP value

OPS-H002.title: MagicLP correctRState Creates Arbitrage Opportunity After Cumulative mtFee Drift
OPS-H002.duplicate_group_description: Permissionless correctRState() creates arbitrage opportunities by resetting PMM curve discontinuously after accumulated target-reserve drift

ECON-H005.title: Reward Token Transfer Failure Permanently Bricks All User Reward Claims
ECON-H005.duplicate_group_description: Non-transferable reward token permanently bricks all reward claims in LockingMultiRewards due to lack of try-catch and no removeReward function

TECH-H008.title: MagicLP.sellShares divCeil target reduction creates cumulative rounding drift degrading LP value
TECH-H008.duplicate_group_description: sellShares uses divCeil for target reduction but floor division for payouts, causing cumulative asymmetric rounding that erodes targets

TECH-H009.title: LockingMultiRewards processExpiredLocks blocks processing lastLockIndex when other locks exist
TECH-H009.duplicate_group_description: processExpiredLocks cannot process the lock at lastLockIndex when other locks exist, creating forced ordering delays

TECH-H013.title: Permissionless sync() lets attackers desync reserves/targets via donation to manipulate pricing and extract value on subsequent swaps
TECH-H013.duplicate_group_description: Permissionless sync() allows attackers to incorporate donated/excess tokens into reserves without target adjustment, enabling pricing manipulation

TECH-H018.title: LockingMultiRewards notifyRewardAmount is vulnerable to fee-on-transfer reward tokens causing reward insolvency and permanent claim reverts
TECH-H018.duplicate_group_description: Fee-on-transfer reward tokens cause LockingMultiRewards insolvency because notifyRewardAmount sets rewardRate based on nominal amount, not actual received

TECH-H015.title: Router multi-hop swaps accept arbitrary path addresses; malicious contracts can steal intermediate tokens because no pool validation is performed
TECH-H015.duplicate_group_description: Router multi-hop swap functions accept arbitrary path addresses without validating they are genuine Factory-registered MagicLP pools

TECH-H019.title: MagicLP ratioSync can permanently revert via division-by-zero when a reserve reaches 0, blocking admin recovery path and leaving pool in broken state
TECH-H019.duplicate_group_description: MagicLP.ratioSync() reverts with division-by-zero when a reserve is 0, removing an admin recovery path

ECON-H006.title: notifyRewardAmount Near Epoch Boundary With Zero TotalSupply Permanently Loses Rewards
ECON-H006.duplicate_group_description: Rewards are permanently lost when notifyRewardAmount is called near epoch boundary and totalSupply is zero during the distribution window

TECH2-H005.title: FeeRateModel getFeeRate passes msg.sender as pool, enabling any caller to spoof 'pool' identity and potentially obtain privileged fee schedules
TECH2-H005.duplicate_group_description: Factory.create uses tx.origin for creator attribution and CREATE2 salt, breaking smart contract wallet composability and causing salt collisions

ECON-H016.title: Precision Loss in Reward Rate Calculation Permanently Locks Dust
ECON-H016.duplicate_group_description: Integer division truncation in notifyRewardAmount permanently strands dust reward tokens in the contract

TECH-H017.title: LockingMultiRewards withdraw/getRewards lack nonReentrant; reward token callbacks (ERC777-style) may reenter and corrupt accounting
TECH-H017.duplicate_group_description: LockingMultiRewards withdraw/getRewards lack reentrancy guard; callback-capable tokens (ERC777-style) could reenter and corrupt accounting

OPS-H007.title: MagicLP Pool Permanently Bricked When Admin Drains Reserve to Zero via setParameters
OPS-H007.duplicate_group_description: Pool permanently bricked when any reserve reaches zero (via admin setParameters, extreme rounding, or other path) — buyShares, swaps, getMidPrice, and Router liquidity all revert

ECON2-H014.title: Math._GeneralIntegrate Overflow Causes DoS for Large Trades on High-I Pools
ECON2-H014.duplicate_group_description: PMM math functions (_GeneralIntegrate, _SolveQuadraticFunctionForTarget, DecimalMath) overflow on large reserves or extreme pool parameters, causing DoS

TECH-H004.title: MagicLP.buyShares allows imbalanced deposits that create reserve-target drift exploitable via arbitrage

TECH-H006.title: Permissionless correctRState enables arbitrage exploitation after systematic mtFee-induced drift

TECH-H007.title: LockingMultiRewards _getRewards reverts for all users if any reward token becomes non-transferable

TECH-H010.title: MagicLP.setParameters truncates pool parameters to smaller types inconsistent with init()

TECH-H011.title: LockingMultiRewards processExpiredLocks is operator-only with no user self-service fallback

TECH-H012.title: LockingMultiRewards.recover allows owner to drain reward token balances without user protection

TECH-H014.title: FlashLoan can be used as a free, zero-fee liquidity primitive when both reserves are restored, enabling external-protocol manipulation at no cost

TECH-H016.title: Router removeLiquidity(ETH) lacks deadline enforcement, enabling execution after user’s intended validity window (stale pricing / stale assumptions)

TECH-H020.title: Factory registry can be poisoned with arbitrary pools (addPool has no validation), enabling UI/indexer deception and misrouting funds

ECON-H007.title: processExpiredLocks lastLockIndex Restriction Delays Fund Unlocking

ECON-H008.title: Fee-on-Transfer Token Breaks LockingMultiRewards Staking Accounting

ECON-H009.title: tx.origin Usage in Fee Rate Determination Enables Fee Bypass via Intermediary Contracts

ECON-H010.title: recover() Function Lacks Reward Token Solvency Guard Enabling Owner to Drain Rewards

ECON-H011.title: setParameters Allows Implementation Owner to Extract Arbitrary Pool Reserves

ECON-H012.title: Maintainer Fee Extraction Causes Systematic Target-Reserve Drift

ECON-H013.title: Flash Loans Can Be Executed Without Paying Fees

ECON-H014.title: Arbitrageurs Can Sweep Pool Excess Balances via Router Swaps

ECON-H015.title: Unsafe Liquidity Addition Results in Permanent Loss of Excess Tokens

ECON-H017.title: Spot Price Flash Loan Manipulation for Oracle Exploitation

ECON-H018.title: Single Paused Reward Token Bricks All Reward Claims

ECON-H019.title: Implementation Owner Can Drain All Pool Reserves

ECON-H020.title: Malicious Implementation Upgrade Can Steal All Swap Outputs

ECON-H021.title: Malicious Operator Can Exhaust User Lock Slots

OPS-H018.title: Dead-end state: LockingMultiRewards has no user-accessible unlock path (operator-only), creating practical fund freeze if ops automation fails
